notes: Spec variant 1: after a clean exit of session S, the agent is re-launched with the same session id. The same UUID reappears (not a new session row); state is ready initially; first turn drives ready→working. Variant 2 (multiple kill+resume across PID lifetimes) is captured as a side-bullet — this recipe records the clean-exit-and-resume case; multi-resume can be a follow-up recipe. The recipe sends a 1-token nudge before exit so claudecode's lazy-transcript materializes (see session-start). The resume step in the driver re-launches with the SAME UUID + cwd so claude appends to the existing transcript.

text: First launch: session appears in ready
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — daemon observes the same UUID's session row coming back in ready after the resume launch
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn'
Second turn completes back to ready (verifies the cycle is independent across lifetimes)
